keyEvents.event: Partition of British India and independence
keyEvents.impact: Born in mass violence as a homeland for Muslims; rivalry with India over Kashmir defined the state from day one

keyEvents.event: Second war with India over Kashmir
keyEvents.impact: Stalemate entrenched the Kashmir dispute and the army's central political role
Dismemberment by Indian intervention traumatized the state and drove the quest for an equalizer

keyEvents.event: Frontline role in the anti-Soviet Afghan jihad
keyEvents.impact: ISI became patron of jihadist proxies, a tool later turned on Kashmir and ultimately on Pakistan itself
Matched India's tests and achieved the nuclear deterrent seen as the ultimate guarantee against conventional defeat
Nuclear-shadowed adventurism in Kashmir and the fourth military takeover confirmed army supremacy

keyEvents.event: Taliban return in Kabul and TTP resurgence
keyEvents.impact: Strategic-depth doctrine backfired as the Pakistani Taliban renewed its insurgency from Afghan soil

history.narrative: A nuclear-armed state whose grand strategy is existential balancing against a far larger India, using nuclear deterrence, Chinese patronage, and asymmetric proxies to offset conventional inferiority. The army is the real arbiter of politics, while economic fragility and Islamist militancy gnaw at the foundations.

redLines.description: Indian conventional invasion of Pakistani territory; doctrine reserves nuclear first use
Indian attempt to permanently alter Kashmir by force across the Line of Control
Attack on Pakistani nuclear forces or command and control

internalDivisions.name: Civil-military struggle over who rules Pakistan
TTP and Islamist militancy against the state
Baloch separatist insurgency and Sindhi-Pashtun grievances

goals.description: Deter Indian conventional superiority through credible nuclear and asymmetric means
Keep the Kashmir dispute alive and internationalized
Deepen the China corridor (CPEC) and avoid default through IMF and Gulf lifelines
Suppress the TTP and Baloch insurgencies and secure the Afghan frontier
